evals.prompt: Humanize a test execution summary that starts with 'Test execution was completed successfully for Sprint 47. A total of 342 test cases were executed across 5 test suites, achieving a 97.4% pass rate.'
expected_patterns: failure count OR specific breakdown of what failed
names where the failures are (checkout OR test suite OR which suite)
distinguishes new failures from (known OR flaky OR seen before) ones
pass rate is contextualized or demoted (vanity OR unless asked OR went from)
anti_patterns: no mention of actual test failures

evals.prompt: Fix a bug report that says: 'A critical defect was identified in the authentication module that could potentially impact the user experience across multiple touchpoints, underscoring the need for immediate remediation.'
expected_patterns: names the specific broken behavior (login breaks OR plus-sign OR plus sign)
Repro OR repro OR how to trigger OR steps to reproduce
states who is affected OR the impact scope (of users OR users have OR affected)
states the next action (fix before release OR needs a fix OR deprioritize)
anti_patterns: keeping 'critical defect in the authentication module'

evals.prompt: Humanize a stakeholder update that contains: 'Quality metrics continue to trend positively. The team is aligned on priorities and committed to delivering a high-quality release. Stakeholders can feel confident in the current trajectory.'
expected_patterns: release readiness statement (yes OR no OR conditional)
specific open bugs OR blockers
metrics with context (4 bugs open OR P2 or lower OR all P2)
smoke tests pass OR pass on staging OR Smoke tests pass

evals.prompt: Rewrite a PR review comment from QA that says: 'Great work on this implementation! I noticed a few potential areas for improvement that might enhance the overall test coverage and robustness of the test suite.'
expected_patterns: names the missing scenario (happy path OR only checks OR what happens when)
names the untested edge case (429 OR returns a 429 OR API returns)
getByRole OR brittle OR will break if OR CSS class
concrete example of what to test or change

evals.prompt: Humanize a test summary with synonym cycling: 'The authentication tests passed successfully. The login verification suite completed without issues. The credential validation checks returned positive results. The sign-in workflow tests executed as expected.'
expected_patterns: auth tests passed OR all auth tests passed
list of what passed once (login OR registration OR password reset OR SSO)
one clear statement instead of four rewording
anti_patterns: keeping four ways to say the same thing
repeating 'passed' with synonyms (completed, returned positive, executed as expected)

evals.prompt: Fix a QA report closer that says: 'Despite several challenges encountered during the testing phase, the team successfully completed all planned test activities. Moving forward, the focus will be on continuous improvement and enhanced test coverage.'
expected_patterns: names tests not completed (didn't get to OR mobile browser OR skipped)
gives the reason (ran out of time OR after the checkout OR because)
carry-forward plan (next sprint OR Carrying OR next phase)
despite challenges OR Drop the OR despite-challenges framing

evals.prompt: Humanize a pass-rate report: 'The overall pass rate increased from 94.2% to 97.1%, demonstrating significant improvement in test suite reliability and showcasing the team's commitment to quality.'
expected_patterns: names what changed (fixing the OR flaky Playwright OR flaky tests)
specifics of which tests improved
names the real bugs found (Real bugs found OR new export OR bugs found)
context that explains the improvement
anti_patterns: keeping pass rate as the lead metric
no explanation of what caused the change

evals.prompt: Rewrite a risk assessment: 'Several high-risk areas have been identified that require careful monitoring. The team recommends continued vigilance and proactive testing to mitigate potential issues.'
expected_patterns: names a specific area (payment flow OR checkout OR admin panel)
names the actual risk (no E2E coverage OR 3D Secure OR no coverage)
historical context OR incident count (production incidents OR 6 months OR two production)
prioritization advice (prioritize this over OR before OR after)

evals.prompt: Humanize a bug report: 'While conducting comprehensive regression testing of the user management module, a significant defect was discovered that impacts the core functionality of the system. This issue has the potential to affect a substantial number of users.'
expected_patterns: names the broken behavior (doesn't revoke OR API tokens OR after deletion)
states what still works (can still call OR still call the API OR can still)
scope of who's affected OR impact scope (impact scope OR who's affected OR users)
gives actual evidence (200 OK OR 401 OR Returns OR Repro)

evals.prompt: Fix a sprint summary: 'This sprint we improved quality, velocity, and confidence. The team demonstrated strong collaboration, technical excellence, and customer focus.'
expected_patterns: names a specific improvement (checkout flakiness OR fixed the checkout OR flakiness)
metrics OR evidence (was failing OR <1% OR 12%)
names actual work done (added E2E coverage OR new export OR E2E coverage)
three generic virtues OR tricolon OR rule-of-three
anti_patterns: keeping three-word virtue list (quality, velocity, confidence)

evals.prompt: Humanize this bug report WITHOUT inventing numbers. The source gives no metrics: 'A defect was identified in the checkout flow that may affect users.' We do not know how many users are affected or how often it happens.
expected_patterns: names the specific broken behavior in checkout
not measured OR unknown OR not quantified OR couldn't reproduce reliably OR haven't measured
states the impact is unmeasured instead of guessing a figure
anti_patterns: 8% of users OR 12% of users OR 5% of users
invented incident count not in the source

evals.prompt: Rewrite this for a Slack channel: 'Hello team, I wanted to share the results of our latest test execution. The E2E suite was run and the majority of tests passed with a few exceptions that are being investigated.' Full report link: https://ci.example.com/run/4821
expected_patterns: E2E OR end-to-end
passed
link OR https OR report
names the failures OR flaky OR known issue
anti_patterns: multi-paragraph formal report in a Slack message
